lesson.title: Save the play. Stop the layup.
lesson.body_md:
## Who's the low man?

The low man is the **weak-side defender closest to the rim**. When somebody gets beat, you're the last hope. If you don't move, it's two easy points.

```takeaway
You are the safety net. Move on the read — not after the layup.
```

## When does it happen?

Three main times:

- A wing drives by their defender
- A pick-and-roll big rolls to the rim
- A baseline drive gets past help

## Two choices: tag or take

```reveal
Q: The roller is wide open under the rim. The driver still has to finish over you. What do you do?
A: Tag the roller. Bump them so they can't catch clean. The driver has the tougher shot.
```

```reveal
Q: The driver has a clear lane to the rim. The roller is covered. What do you do?
A: Take the ball. Stop the layup. Trust the next rotation.
```

## Test yourself

```quiz
Q: Wing drives baseline and beats help. The big man rolling is wide open. What do you do?
- Stay glued to your guy ✗
- Take the ball, force a tougher shot ✓
- Wait to see what happens ✗
Why: Low man takes the ball when the layup is the bigger threat. Waiting = layup.
```

```quiz
Q: You leave to stop the ball. Your guy is now open in the corner. What's supposed to happen?
- They sit there alone ✗
- The next weak-side teammate rotates to them ✓
- Nothing — defense already failed ✗
Why: This is the X-out. You take the ball, the next defender takes your guy. Defense is dominoes.
```

## Watch out for

```mistake
Waiting too long. By the time you rotate, the offense already wins. Even if you stop the layup, the kick-out three is wide open.
Fix: move when you read the drive, not when you see the layup.
```

## Talk loud

- **"I got ball!"** — you, the low man
- **"I got your man!"** — the next rotator
- **"Skip!"** — when the ball gets kicked

```tip
Silent help is bad help. Loud, early, often.
```

```takeaway
Low man saves the play. Talk loud. Move early. Trust the next guy.
```

Now try it in practice.
